recipe.title: Lamb & red wine stew
recipe.description: This slow-cooked lamb stew recipe is so simple to prepare and gives consistently good results. If you don’t want a straight-up casserole, or are looking for ways to use up leftovers,  I’ve given you two topping variations

steps.descr: Trim the ends off the celery and roughly chop the sticks
steps.instructions: Trim the ends off the celery and roughly chop the sticks. Peel and roughly chop the onions. Peel the carrots, slice them lengthways and roughly chop.

on_enter.say: Trim the ends off the celery and roughly chop the sticks. Peel and roughly chop the onions. Peel the carrots, slice them lengthways and roughly chop.

steps.descr: Put a casserole pan on a medium heat
steps.instructions: Put a casserole pan on a medium heat. Put all the vegetables, bay and rosemary into the pan with 2 tablespoons of olive oil and fry for 10 minutes.

on_enter.say: Put a casserole pan on a medium heat. Put all the vegetables, bay and rosemary into the pan with 2 tablespoons of olive oil and fry for 10 minutes.

steps.descr: Dice the lamb into roughly 2cm cubes (if it isn’t...
steps.instructions: Dice the lamb into roughly 2cm cubes (if it isn’t already), then add to the pan with the flour.

on_enter.say: Dice the lamb into roughly 2cm cubes (if it isn’t already), then add to the pan with the flour.

steps.descr: Pour in the wine and tomatoes
steps.instructions: Pour in the wine and tomatoes. Give it a good stir, then season with a pinch of sea salt and a few grinds of black pepper.

on_enter.say: Pour in the wine and tomatoes. Give it a good stir, then season with a pinch of sea salt and a few grinds of black pepper.

steps.descr: Bring to the boil, put the lid on and cook in the oven...
steps.instructions: Bring to the boil, put the lid on and cook in the oven for 2 hours 30 minutes, or until the meat is tender and delicious, removing the lid for the final 30 minutes and adding a splash of water if it looks too dry. (You could also simmer over a medium-low heat on your hob if you prefer.)

on_enter.say: Bring to the boil, put the lid on and cook in the oven for 2 hours 30 minutes, or until the meat is tender and delicious, removing the lid for the final 30 minutes and adding a splash of water if it looks too dry. (You could also simmer over a medium-low heat on your hob if you prefer.)

steps.descr: Remove the bay leaf and rosemary stalks, season to...
steps.instructions: Remove the bay leaf and rosemary stalks, season to taste, then serve. Or finish it off with one of these toppings…

on_enter.say: Remove the bay leaf and rosemary stalks, season to taste, then serve. Or finish it off with one of these toppings…

steps.descr: TO MAKE THE DUMPLINGS Preheat or turn the oven up to...
steps.instructions: TO MAKE THE DUMPLINGS Preheat or turn the oven up to 190°C/375°F/gas 5.

on_enter.say: TO MAKE THE DUMPLINGS Preheat or turn the oven up to 190°C/375°F/gas 5.

steps.descr: Put the flour and a pinch of sea salt and black pepper...
steps.instructions: Put the flour and a pinch of sea salt and black pepper into a bowl, then grate in the cold butter. Using your fingers, gently rub in the butter until it begins to resemble breadcrumbs. Then add a splash of cold water to help bind it into a dough.

on_enter.say: Put the flour and a pinch of sea salt and black pepper into a bowl, then grate in the cold butter. Using your fingers, gently rub in the butter until it begins to resemble breadcrumbs. Then add a splash of cold water to help bind it into a dough.

steps.descr: Divide the dough into 12 pieces and gently roll each...
steps.instructions: Divide the dough into 12 pieces and gently roll each into a round dumpling. The dumplings will suck up quite a bit of moisture, so if your stew looks dry, add a cup of boiling water and give it a good stir.

on_enter.say: Divide the dough into 12 pieces and gently roll each into a round dumpling. The dumplings will suck up quite a bit of moisture, so if your stew looks dry, add a cup of boiling water and give it a good stir.

steps.descr: Place the dumplings on top of your stew and press down...
steps.instructions: Place the dumplings on top of your stew and press down lightly, so they’re half submerged. Cook in the oven, or on the hob over a medium heat, with the lid on for 30 minutes.

on_enter.say: Place the dumplings on top of your stew and press down lightly, so they’re half submerged. Cook in the oven, or on the hob over a medium heat, with the lid on for 30 minutes.

steps.descr: TO MAKE SHEPHERD'S PIE Preheat or turn the oven up to...
steps.instructions: TO MAKE SHEPHERD'S PIE Preheat or turn the oven up to 190°C/375°F/gas 5. Transfer your cooked stew to a large baking dish.

on_enter.say: TO MAKE SHEPHERD'S PIE Preheat or turn the oven up to 190°C/375°F/gas 5. Transfer your cooked stew to a large baking dish.

steps.descr: Peel the potatoes, cut them in half into even-sized...
steps.instructions: Peel the potatoes, cut them in half into even-sized pieces, and put them into a pan of salted, boiling water. Boil for about 10 minutes or until tender, then drain, steam dry and return them to the pan.

on_enter.say: Peel the potatoes, cut them in half into even-sized pieces, and put them into a pan of salted, boiling water. Boil for about 10 minutes or until tender, then drain, steam dry and return them to the pan.

steps.descr: Add the milk, butter and a pinch of salt and pepper,...
steps.instructions: Add the milk, butter and a pinch of salt and pepper, then mash until smooth and creamy, adding another splash of milk if necessary.

on_enter.say: Add the milk, butter and a pinch of salt and pepper, then mash until smooth and creamy, adding another splash of milk if necessary.

steps.descr: Roughly top the stew with the mashed potato (don’t worry...
steps.instructions: Roughly top the stew with the mashed potato (don’t worry about it being smooth and even), pick over a few rosemary leaves and lightly push them into the potato.

on_enter.say: Roughly top the stew with the mashed potato (don’t worry about it being smooth and even), pick over a few rosemary leaves and lightly push them into the potato.

steps.descr: Drizzle with some olive oil, lightly coating the...
steps.instructions: Drizzle with some olive oil, lightly coating the rosemary leaves, then cook in the oven for 25 minutes, or until golden and bubbling.

on_enter.say: Drizzle with some olive oil, lightly coating the rosemary leaves, then cook in the oven for 25 minutes, or until golden and bubbling.
